text:
In parallel to Israel’s spiraling war with Iran, the Israeli military has not stopped attacking Gaza, where it fought Hamas, the Palestinian armed group, in a devastating two-year war. On Sunday, at least eight police officers affiliated with the Hamas-controlled administration in the territory were killed in a strike, the Gazan interior ministry said in a statement. The Israeli military did not immediately comment on the incident.
The Israeli military has accused the Palestinian militant group of violating the cease-fire the two sides reached late last year and continued to bombard the territory. While backed by Iran, Hamas has not joined the regional war, unlike Hezbollah, an Iran-supported militia in Lebanon.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Sunday there were “no guarantees” that oil prices would fall in the coming weeks, despite President Trump’s having vowed repeatedly that the United States will try to force Iran to stop attacks on oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.
title: Iran War Live Updates: ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Drop Soon, U.S. Official Says

citability.title: Iran War Live Updates: ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Drop Soon, U.S. Official Says
